metadata.summary: On October 15, 2023, 12-year-old Arjun Mehta, son of prominent Noida-based real estate developer Vikram Mehta, was kidnapped from outside his school by two masked individuals. A ransom demand of ₹2.5 crore was sent via encrypted messaging app, with instructions to deliver cash at a remote forested area near Sector 57. The family immediately filed an FIR at Noida Police Station (PS Sector 16). Call records show three suspicious calls from a burner phone to Vikram’s mobile, all timed between 3:15 PM and 4:45 PM on the day of the kidnapping. GPS data from Arjun’s smartwatch indicates he was last seen near the school gate, then moved toward the Noida-Greater Noida Expressway before the signal dropped. A recovery operation led by Special Task Force (STF) on October 18 resulted in Arjun’s rescue near an abandoned warehouse in Sector 62, unharmed but traumatized. However, the kidnappers escaped. Five suspects were identified: Vikram’s estranged brother Rohan Mehta, his business rival Rajiv Khanna, the school’s former security head Manoj Verma, Vikram’s personal driver Ramesh Kumar, and a known local extortionist, Bittu Singh. Six witnesses provided statements, but three have since recanted or changed their accounts under pressure. Forensic analysis of the ransom note revealed traces of a rare ink used only in government offices — a clue that appears deliberately misleading. The case is further complicated by a forged bank transfer receipt allegedly showing Rohan Mehta withdrew ₹20 lakh the day before the kidnapping — a document later found to be digitally altered. Multi-hop reasoning is required to connect the burner phone’s last known location to a stolen motorcycle, which was traced to a pawnshop owned by a relative of the school principal — a connection no one has yet made.

hidden_ground_truth.true_motive: Manoj Verma sought revenge for being wrongfully terminated from his security job after he exposed Vikram Mehta’s illegal land acquisition in Sector 45. Ramesh provided access to Arjun’s school schedule and vehicle routes. Priya, Vikram’s estranged wife and Manoj’s secret lover, forged the bank receipt to frame Rohan and diverted police attention.
true_timeline: 2023-10-15 14:30: Manoj Verma, using a stolen uniform, enters school premises under guise of maintenance check.
2023-10-15 14:55: Ramesh Kumar parks Vikram’s SUV near the school gate as a decoy; Arjun is lured toward it by a fake message from his mother.
2023-10-15 15:02: Arjun is forcibly taken into the SUV, driven to a hidden garage in Sector 58.
2023-10-15 15:30: Manoj and Ramesh switch vehicles using a stolen motorcycle registered to Bittu Singh’s cousin.
2023-10-15 16:10: Ransom message sent from a burner phone purchased with cash at a shop in Ghaziabad.
2023-10-15 17:00: Priya Mehta uses her access to Vikram’s office printer to forge the ₹20 lakh withdrawal receipt, printing it on official stationery.
2023-10-16 03:00: Arjun is moved to the abandoned warehouse in Sector 62, where he is held until rescue.
2023-10-18 11:20: STF raids warehouse after tracking the burner phone’s last ping to a nearby tower, but suspects flee via a pre-planned tunnel to a farmhouse in Ballabhgarh.
forged_evidence: Bank withdrawal receipt dated 2023-10-14 showing ₹20 lakh withdrawal from Rohan Mehta’s account — forged by Priya Mehta using Vikram’s office printer and stolen bank letterhead.
Security camera footage from Sector 57 showing a man matching Rohan’s build near the ransom drop site — digitally altered to insert Rohan’s face using AI.

injected_contradictions.description: Witness (school janitor) claims to have seen Arjun being taken by a man in a blue jacket — but the actual kidnappers wore black. The blue jacket was worn by a school teacher who was later bribed to lie.
Ramesh Kumar’s phone logs show he was at home at 15:00, but tower data places him near the school — the discrepancy is due to a spoofed location via a VPN app installed by Priya.
The ransom note’s ink matches government office supplies, but the printer used was never in any government office — it was a high-end printer in Vikram’s private office, which Priya accessed daily.
Bittu Singh claims his motorcycle was stolen on October 13, but CCTV from the pawnshop shows it was returned on October 16 by Ramesh Kumar — a fact omitted in his statement.
Vikram claims he never spoke to Rohan in months — but call records show two calls from Vikram’s personal phone to Rohan’s unlisted number on October 12, both deleted from his device.

Arjun’s smartwatch shows no movement after 15:10, but forensic analysis reveals the GPS module was manually disabled at 15:05 — not by the kidnappers, but by Ramesh Kumar using a device he kept from his old job as a vehicle tracker technician.

contradictions.claim_a: Rohan Mehta was at his farmhouse in Ballabhgarh during the kidnapping (15:00–16:30), as confirmed by his wife and two guests.
contradictions.claim_b: Cell tower data from Rohan’s phone shows it was active near Sector 57 at 15:45 — the exact time of the ransom drop — but the phone was stolen two days prior and used by Ramesh Kumar.

contradictions.claim_a: School janitor, Ram Singh, saw a man in a blue jacket force Arjun into a car near the gate.
contradictions.claim_b: Security footage from the school’s main gate shows the kidnapper wore black, and the only person in blue was teacher Anjali Rao, who was in her classroom at the time.

contradictions.claim_a: The ransom note was printed on paper with ink traceable to Uttar Pradesh Revenue Department printers.
contradictions.claim_b: The same ink was found in Vikram Mehta’s private office printer, which Priya Mehta accessed daily — and the paper stock matches the office’s unused supply.

contradictions.claim_a: Rohan Mehta withdrew ₹20 lakh from his account on October 14, suggesting he funded the kidnapping.
contradictions.claim_b: Bank records show the withdrawal was never made — the receipt is a forgery printed on Vikram’s office printer using stolen letterhead.

contradictions.claim_a: Arjun’s smartwatch GPS signal stopped at 15:10, indicating he was taken at that time.
contradictions.claim_b: Forensic analysis shows the GPS module was manually disabled at 15:05 using a Bluetooth signal from a device Ramesh Kumar used as a vehicle tracker — meaning the kidnapping occurred before the signal dropped.

contradictions.claim_a: The burner phone used for ransom calls was purchased in Ghaziabad on October 14 by a man matching Bittu Singh’s description.
contradictions.claim_b: CCTV from the Ghaziabad shop shows the buyer was wearing a cap and mask — but the height and gait match Ramesh Kumar, not Bittu Singh.

contradictions.claim_a: Bittu Singh claims his motorcycle was stolen on October 13 and never recovered.
contradictions.claim_b: Pawnshop owner in Sector 62 confirms the motorcycle was returned on October 16 by a man matching Ramesh Kumar’s build, who claimed it was 'found' and wanted to claim reward.

contradictions.claim_a: Vikram Mehta says he had no contact with Rohan for over a year.
contradictions.claim_b: Call logs from Vikram’s deleted call history (recovered via forensic imaging) show two calls to Rohan’s unlisted number on October 12 — both lasting under 30 seconds.

contradictions.claim_a: Arjun’s school bag was found near the warehouse with no fingerprints except his own.
contradictions.claim_b: Trace DNA from a hair strand on the bag’s strap matches Priya Mehta — she claims she never touched the bag, but she helped pack it the morning of the kidnapping.

contradictions.claim_a: Ramesh Kumar’s wife says he was home all evening on October 15, watching TV.
contradictions.claim_b: Smart TV logs show no activity between 14:30 and 17:00 — the device was powered off, but the remote was used to turn it on at 17:05 — suggesting someone faked the alibi.
